mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when the policy rate is falling, and it fails when rate cuts occur after risk assets have already priced recession.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in most episodes where FEDFUNDS is falling for two consecutive months, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which precedes lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest once unemployment is clearly accelerating, not merely high.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broader final demand and housing-linked spending are firming, which supports near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if retail sales improve while housing collapses, implying a narrow or unsustainable demand impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improvement in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if positive returns fail to dominate in the top signal quartile.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling unemployment reflects strengthening real activity rather than a demand squeeze, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when production gains are not accompanied by a spike in inflation or policy tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO with flat or lower UNRATE fails to yield positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high but stable CPIAUCSL combined with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies real rates remain suppressed, which is supportive for equities over the next week through discount-rate relief. The signal fails when inflation accelerates enough to force imminent tightening expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates > 0.2pp above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered episodes do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if inflation surprises do not interact with FEDFUNDS in the expected direction.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS that remains positive while UNRATE stops rising indicates labor-market stabilization, which should lift risk sentiment and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if payroll gains do not translate into lower recession odds.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE is flat or down over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor stabilization condition does not precede positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling GDPC1 trend signals weakening aggregate demand and lower expected earnings, which should pressure the S&P 500 over the following quarter. The signal is cleanest when quarterly real GDP momentum turns negative and remains negative.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above recent lows, together with steady retail sales, indicate that rate-sensitive demand is no longer deteriorating, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when housing rebounds are purely inventory-driven and not matched by household spending.
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound triggers do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if RSAFS does not improve the hit rate relative to HOUST alone.
